provenance.source_corpus: Response of a Small Mammal Population Postremediation for an Accidental Bitumen and Steam Release from a Wellhead in the Athabasca Oil Sands

nodes.name: biodegradation, photolysis, and other natural processes

source_spans.text: hepatic detoxification essentially returned to background /reference levels by 2017, providing evidence that toxicity from the bitumen‐related compounds decreased over time through biodegradation, photolysis, and other natural processes.

hepatic detoxification essentially returned to background /reference levels by 2017, providing evidence that toxicity from the bitumen‐related compounds decreased over time through biodegradation, photolysis, and other natural processes.

Although a significant increase in cytochrome P450 (CYP1A1; hepatic detoxification) activity was observed in specimens collected in the high‐impact zones when compared to reference sites, which is indicative of exposure to contaminants, it was not correlated with any observable adverse biological outcome (Berger et al. 2016).

Although a significant increase in cytochrome P450 (CYP1A1; hepatic detoxification) activity was observed in specimens collected in the high‐impact zones when compared to reference sites, which is indicative of exposure to contaminants, it was not correlated with any observable adverse biological outcome (Berger et al. 2016).
Lead residues appeared to negatively affect body condition, a correlation that differed between years. In 2014, Pb was negatively correlated with body condition considering all animals
Lead residues appeared to negatively affect body condition, a correlation that differed between years. In 2014, Pb was negatively correlated with body condition considering all animals
In 2014 Sb was marginally higher (p = 0.06), whereas Cr was significantly higher ( p < 0.03) than in the reference‐site animals and SMI was positively correlated with Cr (p = 0.027)
